data_contracts_notes: No schema/field changes described. Configuration parameter `max_persist_retries` mentioned but no type, range, or validation rules specified.
outcome_clarity_notes: Clear that retry logic is being added to TaskRunRecorder with up to N retries before dropping events. However, ambiguous: (1) what 'dropped' means operationally (silent? logged? metrics?), (2) whether this only applies to `handle_task_run_events` or broader, (3) retry backoff strategy beyond the 1.5s sleep mentioned in tests.
error_states_notes: Partially present. Specifies behavior on transient DB failures (re-queue, retry). Missing: (1) which specific exceptions trigger retry vs immediate drop, (2) what happens if event is malformed, (3) timeout behavior, (4) circuit breaker or degradation patterns if DB stays down.
scope_boundaries_notes: Good: clearly scoped to TaskRunRecorder's `handle_task_run_events`. Minor gap: doesn't explicitly state whether this affects event ordering guarantees or delivery semantics.
acceptance_criteria_notes: Weak. Has a human checklist (retry semantics, flakiness, test coverage) but no formal test cases. Missing: (1) no Given/When/Then, (2) no specific expected behavior statement like 'system recovers after 3 consecutive DB failures', (3) metrics/observability criteria undefined.
dependency_context_notes: Good references: PR #19586, issue #18753, PR #19710. Missing: (1) file path to TaskRunRecorder, (2) which database client/ORM is being called, (3) whether Redis or other infrastructure is involved beyond what linked issues suggest.
behavioral_specificity_notes: Moderate. Specified: max_persist_retries default=5, 1 retry on failure (with 1.5s sleep in tests), events are 'dropped' after max. Vague: (1) is 1.5s sleep the actual retry delay or just for tests?, (2) is retry deterministic or exponential?, (3) what 'transient' means—which error codes/types qualify?

data_contracts_notes: env var structure is shown (name/value pairs) but no schema or type definitions provided

PRESENT: Clear bug reproduction: env vars set in work pool appear duplicated in job manifest
Concrete example of the problem (TEST_VALUE appears twice)
Root cause identified: _populate_env_in_manifest() doesn't distinguish between template-rendered and hardcoded vars
Solution approach: filter template_env to remove duplicates by name matching transformed_env
PARTIALLY_PRESENT: Solution description is incomplete (cut off mid-sentence at 'don't confl')
Behavioral specificity: the deduplication logic is described but implementation detail is truncated
Acceptance criteria: implied (no duplicates) but not formally stated as test cases
Scope boundaries: unclear if this affects only work pools or also other deployment methods
ABSENT: No explicit acceptance criteria/test cases (Given/When/Then format)
No error handling scenarios described (what if env var names are invalid, what if malformed manifest)
No discussion of edge cases: empty env lists, null values, special characters in names
No field-level data contract for env var structure (type requirements, length limits, charset)
